programmes.show_name: Not Going Out: The True Meaning of Christmas
programmes.show_description: Lee buys the perfect Christmas present for his children, but their grandparents have the same idea.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: An extended episode of the panel show. Guest host Jo Brand is joined by panellists Rylan Clark-Neal and journalist Robert Peston in taking a sideways look at the news.
The magnificent Lou Sanders introduces comedians Celya AB and Neil Delamere to the stage.
Maisie Adam introduces comedians Michael Odewale and Susie McCabe to the stage.
Rise and Shine: Richard Ayoade tests gadgets to get people get out of bed and out of the house in the morning with long-term early riser and DJ Sara Cox.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Food Pairings: Si and Dave explore how British ingredients can combine to make classic pairings. The duo create a pork and apple dish and a pear and stilton tart.
Newcastle: Not everything is black and white in Newcastle, as savvy bidders gamble on unseen storage units in the hit reality show. Feisty new girl Ione clashes with Shaun.
Northampton: Britain's answer to the riotous reality series. Ione goes head to head with Sam. And who will be left as a bridesmaid when a wedding carriage comes up for grabs?
Actor Steve Coogan joins the trio as they take a gander at the Aston Martin Vanquish, the Ferrari 575, the Nissan Skyline and cars from the Bond film Die Another Day.
Chat show king Jonathan Ross guests as Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and Jason Dawe review the S Class Mercedes, the Audi A8, the Jaguar XJ and the Bentley Arnage T.
Simon Reeve travels through Texas and Mexico and joins the US Border Patrol - who pick up migrants who have illegally crossed the Rio Grande river in search of a new life - on the way.
In the fifth and final leg of his journey, Simon travels across Central America from the Caribbean to the Pacific by beginning in Belize and finishing his trip in Costa Rica.
Bob Mortimer returns to his childhood roots in Middlesbrough to fish the Rivers Esk and Tees with his dear friend and angling mentor Paul Whitehouse.
Pies: The hairy duo celebrate British food. They find out who ate all the pies on the football pitch and discover the story behind some of the nation's best loved pies.
Takeaways: The biker duo explore how Britain's love affair with takeaway food has evolved and cook some of their favourites in the Best of British kitchen.
Robson is joined by his uncle and nephew on a mission to try new activities in the great outdoors that include birdwatching and gliding.
Simon Reeve travels through Texas and Mexico and joins the US Border Patrol - who pick up migrants who have illegally crossed the Rio Grande river in search of a new life - on the way.
In the fifth and final leg of his journey, Simon travels across Central America from the Caribbean to the Pacific by beginning in Belize and finishing his trip in Costa Rica.

programmes.show_name: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programmes.show_description: Operation Safeguard: A nationwide investigation begins when Dorset Police uncover an international gang posing as couriers to scam vulnerable pensioners out of thousands of pounds.

programmes.show_name: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programmes.show_description: Operation Renaissance: Arson leads to a gunfight as Merseyside Police respond to two terrifying attacks on the Wirral and escalating violence results in a gang member being hunted.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: An extended episode of the panel show. Guest host Jo Brand is joined by panellists Rylan Clark-Neal and journalist Robert Peston in taking a sideways look at the news.
Reflections: Sandi Toksvig reflects on this and that in an episode themed around reflections with Alan Davies, Zoe Lyons, Joe Lycett and Liza Tarbuck.
George Osborne's weird Tory conference stance is meat and drink to savage panel show satirists Hugh Dennis, Rob Beckett, Ed Byrne, Milton Jones, Ed Gamble and Holly Walsh.
The panel show's obligatory end-of-season special with fresh material and token bloopers. Luckily for us, there's no filler and all killer satire, silliness and stand-up.
The wonderfully witty Ellie Taylor introduces rising comedy star Fin Taylor and the provocative Tez Ilyas to the stage.
Knits and Knots: Sue Perkins, Ross Noble, David Mitchell and Alan Davies tie themselves up in knots as the all-knowing Stephen Fry quizzes them on knitting, katydids and knackers!
Love Letters: More laugh-out-loud humour with the dysfunctional Rose family. Moira loses sleep stressing over old love letters, and David and Stevie get held up at the store.
The Plant: More sneaky shenanigans from the quirkily lovable sitcom. A 'plant' causes mayhem when Alexis secures a reservation at the motel for an influential travel blogger.
The Dress: More slick comedy from the town with a highly novel name. Johnny is hit with a couple of unexpected bills at the motel that clash with Moira's need for a pricey dress.
Housewarming: Entertaining comedy from across the pond. Patrick hosts a housewarming party but offends David when he refuses to let him help with the preparations for the big bash.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Cheese: The bikers explore how Britain's love affair with cheese has evolved over centuries, and discover that the original cheesecake recipes were British.
Gwent Police stumble upon a multi-million-pound drug operation which takes an unexpected turn when detectives unravel a cryptocurrency money laundering scheme.
A mysterious series of vehicle fires break out across Bristol as fire crews battle 40 blazes - elsewhere, police in Durham examine a dangerous flat fire.
Gearbox Overhaul: A maintenance team attempts to overhaul a finicky gearbox while another team must shunt an electric passenger train across multiple rail crossings during peak hour.
Yucatan: Rick's journey begins to draw to a close as he heads east from Oaxaca to the Yucatan Peninsula, a place once frequented by real pirates of the Caribbean and our own Sir Francis Drake.
Officers find a suspicious compartment within the trailer of a truck returning to Texas, and a man hides over 300 cheap forms of medication about his person in Progreso.
A pedestrian is caught at Hidalgo Bridge smuggling narcotics in bags of coffee, and a nervous woman in Progreso is taken for a pat down.
World's Fastest Toboggan: The motorbike racer and mechanic sets out to break the record for the world's fastest gravity-powered sled at the Pista Riberal speed ski slope in the Pyrenees.
The manic motoring magazine returns as Messrs Clarkson, Hammond and May take on a hatchback challenge. Downton Abbey's Hugh Bonneville is the Star in the Reasonably Priced Car.
The manic motoring series continues with a jam-packed show featuring Italian sports cars and a trip to Belgium. Tom Hiddleston puts in a swift lap in the Reasonably Priced Car.
Dinner for Two: The Bikers show how to make a dinner for two that is fit for a king on a pauper's budget, and chef Oliver Limousin gives them world-class presentation tips.
Yucatan: Rick's journey begins to draw to a close as he heads east from Oaxaca to the Yucatan Peninsula, a place once frequented by real pirates of the Caribbean and our own Sir Francis Drake.
The Unseen Bits: Rob Brydon hosts a special compilation episode of the comedy panel show featuring classic and unseen bits.
Antiques expert Raj Bisram, all-round entertainer Josie Lawrence, comedian Mark Watson, and TV presenter Laura Whitmore grace the Pointless friend's show with their quizzing presence.
Comedians Kae Kurd, Zoe Lyons, Andrew Hunter Murray and Kate Robbins hope to see the funny side - and win a prize or two - when they take on the Pointless friend's fiendish games.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: More extended highlights from the much-loved panel show. Guest host Bill Bailey is joined by panellists Fin Taylor and Dawn Butler MP in taking a swipe at near-recent events.
New. As mankind continues to hit snooze on the Doomsday Clock, Frankie returns to make a string of bold and often outrageous statements and pick them apart with the help of his guests.
Knits and Knots: Sue Perkins, Ross Noble, David Mitchell and Alan Davies tie themselves up in knots as the all-knowing Stephen Fry quizzes them on knitting, katydids and knackers!
